title: Prophecy Dooms
entries: giants will rise from the sea and trample cities and mountains to dust.
as oceans boil and skies shatter, plunging the world into eternal darkness
when the very fabric of time unravels, and reality trembles
with lands sinking, leaving nothing but vast, unfathomable voids
as fire engulfs all, and ash becomes the only remnant of a once-thriving world
with the deafening silence of a world devoid of life and joy
the stars will rain down, burning away the forests and the seas.
the barrier between worlds will wear thin and tear, merging a corner of the multiverse into a mind-bending mosaic.
light will fade from the heavens and all the world will become the larder of monsters swarming up from its shadow.
an army of hollowed men will be raised, wielding weapons yet unseen to scourge the earth clean.
the moon will crack as if an egg and release unclean radiance that transmutes all things exposed to it.
a slow and steady plague of petrification will sweep the globe. Refugees from stone nations will hack up sand from bloody lungs, all triumphs lost to Ozymandian crumbling.
complexity will flatten until only an infinite featureless plane exists. Higher thought will be one of the first things to go.
things will begin to decay while still living, the only way to stave it off for a time being to devour another living thing.
the world will be swallowed by a tempest. The land will be drowned by sky-high waves and vapourized beneath volleys of lightning.
all water will become bitter and undrinkable, the soil will lose its goodness, and the glare of the sun will intensify until everywhere is a bleached desert.
all people will become utterly atomized and alienated from each other, irrevocably separated into personal realities that can only contact each other through horrific violence.
a veil will be lifted, and everything up until that point will seem to have been very silly. People will laugh themselves to death, or not bother with anything further until it all falls apart.
every pantheon’s Götterdämmerung will kick off at once.
the perfect cancer will metastasize, an immortal disease that assimilates all life within it.
the meek shall inherit the earth, and the strong shall devour the meek’s reward.
fire will spread from the world’s core, and ice from its poles. Everything will be destroyed in their collision.
sense and causality will dissolve as if all the world were a dream.
sorcerous ability will appear at random in people, in frequency and potency never before seen. Their magically-backed whims will tear reality asunder.
the tools and machines of humanity will sprout new forms and complexities at an accelerating rate, coating first the planet and then the sun, ushering in the obsolescence of mere meat.
celestial bodies will dance erratically across the heavens, signaling the great cosmic ballet's end.
ancient sleeping titans below the earth's crust will awaken in anguish, reshaping continents.
oceans will turn thick and inky, and monstrous leviathans will emerge, devouring any ship that dares sail.
time will warp, fast-forwarding the life and decay of civilizations in mere moments.
gravity itself will falter, leading to vast floating landmasses and unpredictable tidal forces.
all reflections will start showing an alternate, nightmarish reality, causing madness in those who gaze for too long.
echoes of past calamities will replay, bringing forth volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, and hurricanes of unprecedented magnitude.
the winds will carry whispers of the departed, sowing chaos and paranoia among the living.
plants will grow rampant, ensnaring cities and suffocating life, turning the world into an overgrown tomb.
vast chasms will open up in the earth, releasing suffocating miasma and drawing down the unwary.
unyielding darkness will envelope the land for days on end, and when it finally lifts, entire regions might have vanished.
births will become rare, while the elderly will not pass on, leading to a world of the old and infirm.
mystical ley lines will blaze with arcane energy, decimating landscapes and altering the very essence of magic.
every spoken word will become tangible, often with catastrophic results.
spectral apparitions will manifest in broad daylight, reenacting their moments of despair and sorrow.
music and harmonies will distort, turning any form of rhythm into a cacophonous dirge.
shadows will gain sentience, rebelling against their casters and revealing their hidden desires and fears.
wildlife will congregate in bizarre rituals, hinting at an intelligence or influence previously unrecognized.
a deafening silence will permeate, punctuated only by the sound of the world's slow, grinding halt.
emotions will manifest as visible auras, leading to pandemonium as true intentions are laid bare for all to see.
all written words, regardless of age, will start to shift and change, creating new histories and truths every day.
mountains will rise and sink at will, as if the world itself breathes a labored breath.
entire constellations will blink out of existence, leaving a starless void in the night sky.
seasons will become unpredictable, with winter’s frost suddenly giving way to summer’s blaze within hours.
an unending rain of ash will darken the skies, heralding a world on fire, yet no flames will be in sight.
food, regardless of its source, will turn to ash in the mouths of those who consume it.
an ethereal, haunting lullaby will play from an unknown source, ensnaring the minds of all who hear it.
crops will grow twisted and sentient, lashing out at those who come near.
stone and metal structures will begin to bleed, and the blood will whisper secrets from times long forgotten.
lakes and rivers will turn crystal clear, and those who peer into their depths will see visions of possible futures.
mystical barriers protecting sacred or forbidden places will dissipate, revealing ancient horrors or treasures.
every dream will become a shared experience, and the waking world will become indistinguishable from the dream realm.
creatures of myth and folklore will return, but not as allies. Instead, they will seek retribution for forgotten wrongs.
an aurora borealis will span across the entire globe, its colors narrating an alien tale.
children will be born with ancient eyes, filled with the wisdom and sorrow of eons.
the dead will refuse to rest, not as undead but as restless spirits seeking a world they no longer recognize.
forests will move and shift overnight, their paths leading travelers to realms unknown.
deserts will become oceans, and oceans will turn to deserts, reversing the world's geography.
ancient ruins will rebuild themselves overnight, restored to their former glory but devoid of life.
clocks and timekeeping devices will simultaneously shatter, marking an era where time has no dominion.
